message:
fix(scheduler): eliminate worker busy-loop when only future-timed tasks are queued

The worker backoff phase used is_empty() to decide whether to break out
of the park loop, but is_empty() returns false for queues containing
future-timed tasks that aren't yet runnable. This caused workers to spin
through the main loop finding nothing to execute.

- Add has_runnable_work(now) to PriorityScheduler and GlobalInjector
  that checks whether any task is actually ready to run at the given
  timestamp, distinguishing runnable from merely-present
- Replace is_empty() checks in the three-lane worker backoff (Phase 6)
  with has_runnable_work(now) to correctly identify when work exists
- Unify park timeout calculation across timer driver, local scheduler
  next_deadline(), and global injector peek_earliest_deadline(), taking
  the minimum to prevent sleeping past a timed task's deadline
- Move fast_queue.is_empty() check before the backoff loop since it is
  single-producer and cannot change while the worker is in backoff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
